product_updates.title: SpaceX IPO: Live updates on everything you need to know
product_updates.content: TechCrunch has followed SpaceX's start, struggles, and successes from the early days. And we're here for what happens next too. This package of SpaceX IPO coverage includes who stands to win (and maybe some who won't), pre-IPO deals, and what's tucked inside its S-1 registration document.

product_updates.title: Meta’s months-old AI unit is a soul-crushing gulag, say the engineers stuck inside it
product_updates.content: A new report suggests the unit, which employs 6,500 people, is on the verge of revolt.

product_updates.title: Chinese cybercrime operation that used AI to scam ‘hundreds of thousands of victims’ sued by Google
product_updates.content: The tech giant said a group called "Outsider Enterprise" used AI to scam hundreds of thousands of victims, sending 2.5 million text messages over a span of two weeks.

product_updates.title: Mistral is rumored to be raising €3B at €20B valuation
product_updates.content: The funding round would value the company at around €20 billion (about $23.15 billion), nearly double its Series C valuation of €11.7 billion.

product_updates.title: SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI’s hot IPO summer
product_updates.content: The IPO market is back, and it’s not the same companies leading the charge. FAANG had a good run, but a new acronym is taking over: MANGOS — Meta (or Microsoft, depending on who you ask), Anthropic, Nvidia, Google, OpenAI, and SpaceX. Half of that bunch is heading to public markets in the same wi…

product_updates.title: It’s hot IPO summer, and the MANGOS are ripe
product_updates.content: The IPO market is back, and it’s not the same companies leading the charge. FAANG had a good run, but a new acronym is taking over: MANGOS — Meta (or Microsoft, depending on who you ask), Anthropic, Nvidia, Google, OpenAI, and SpaceX. Half of that bunch is heading to public markets in the same wi…

product_updates.title: Cheaper, faster, and culturally aware, Avataar’s video AI is built for India’s scale
product_updates.content: Avataar AI's distilled video model is priced at $0.005 for every second of generation.

product_updates.title: Theker just raised $85M to build the factory robot that doesn’t specialize in anything
product_updates.content: Unlike humanoid robots designed around a fixed form — think Boston Dynamics — Theker's machines are built to be reconfigured.

product_updates.title: Jeff Bezos’s Prometheus raises $12B to build an ‘artificial general engineer’ for the physical world
product_updates.content: The new round values the physical AI startup that aims to automate heavy engineering and drug design at $41 billion.

technical_breakthroughs.title: PeopleSoft 0-day affecting hundreds of organizations steals gigabytes of data
technical_breakthroughs.content: Vulnerability in the Oracle-owned PeopleSoft software is about as critical as they come.

technical_breakthroughs.title: You do your own time
technical_breakthroughs.content: There we were, a regular murderers’ row of librarians. Little Jo. Eustace. And me. technical_breakthroughs.title: Why “reprogramming” is the buzziest approach to reversing aging right now
technical_breakthroughs.content: Earlier this week, Life Biosciences, a biotech company focused on reversing age-related diseases, announced that it had dosed its first volunteer. A person with glaucoma has had an experimental treatment injected straight into their eyeball. The idea is to try to treat the disease—which can cause…

technical_breakthroughs.content: MIT Technology Review Explains: Let our writers untangle the complex, messy world of science and technology to help you understand what’s coming next.
